data.name: AppDescription
data.value: Review file and folder context-menu entries, toggle them safely, and review new additions detected by the backend service.

data.name: UpdateAvailableMessage
data.value: Version {0} is available on GitHub Releases.

data.name: SystemServiceRequiredBanner
data.value: System service is not installed. Open Settings to install it.

data.name: SystemServiceUnavailableBanner
data.value: System service is unavailable. Open Settings to install or repair it.

data.name: MonitoredCategoriesDescription
data.value: The backend watches common HKCR shell-verb and shell-extension roots for files, folders, directories, backgrounds, drives, libraries, and all file-system objects.

data.name: PendingApprovalDescription
data.value: Newly detected items appear here in a disabled state so you can allow them, keep them disabled, or remove them.

data.name: ServiceNotesDescription
data.value: The backend runs as a Windows Service. Shell verbs are toggled through Windows shell visibility flags, and shell extensions are blocked through the standard Shell Extensions\Blocked registry path.

data.name: ApprovalRemovePrompt
data.value: Remove {0} from the pending-review queue and delete its registry item?

data.name: NoNotes
data.value: No command text or CLSID metadata was captured for this entry.

data.name: LibraryCategoryDescription
data.value: HKCR\LibraryFolder, HKCR\LibraryFolder\Background, and HKCR\UserLibraryFolder shell roots

data.name: ConsistencyIssueGeneric
data.value: The current registry state does not match the app's saved state.

data.name: StartupChangeNotificationFormat
data.value: Detected an external change for {1} in the {0} category.

data.name: RegistryMissingText
data.value: This item is currently missing from the registry.

data.name: PendingApprovalSystemNotificationTitle
data.value: New context menu item pending review

data.name: PendingApprovalSystemNotificationMessage
data.value: {0} was added to the review queue. Open the app to review it.

data.name: ApprovalRemoveWithoutRegistryConfirmation
data.value: This review item no longer exists in the registry. It will only be removed from the review list.

data.name: ApprovalRemoveConfirmation
data.value: This review item still exists in the registry. Confirming will only remove it from the review queue; delete it from its page if you also want to remove the menu entry.

data.name: ServiceMissingPrompt
data.value: The backend service is not installed. Install and start it with administrator privileges now?

data.name: ServiceRepairPrompt
data.value: The backend service is installed but not reachable. Repair or start it with administrator privileges now?

data.name: ServiceInstallSucceeded
data.value: The backend service is installed and running.

data.name: ServiceInstallFailed
data.value: The backend service could not be installed or started: {0}

data.name: ServiceInstallFailedDetailed
data.value: The backend service could not be installed or started. Details: {0}

data.name: ServiceNotRunningStatus
data.value: The backend service is installed, but it is not running. Current status: {0}

data.name: ServicePipeUnavailableStatus
data.value: The backend service started, but the IPC pipe is still unavailable. Service status: {0}

data.name: ServiceBootstrapInProgress
data.value: Installing or repairing the backend service...

data.name: BackendExecutableMissing
data.value: Could not find ContextMenuManagerPlus.Service.exe. Build or publish the backend first.

data.name: ServiceOperationCancelled
data.value: The elevated install/start operation was cancelled.

data.name: TrayBackgroundInfoText
data.value: The app is minimized to the system tray and keeps watching backend notifications.

data.name: ProtectedOpenItemPrompt
data.value: This item is the "Open" menu for files or folders. Changing it blindly may prevent files or folders from opening. Continue anyway? (Not recommended)

data.name: PermanentDeletePrompt
data.value: Permanently remove the backup for {0}? This action cannot be undone.

data.name: DeleteOperationTimeoutDetail
data.value: The operation timed out and may have been blocked by security software.

data.name: AutoStartOnLoginDescription
data.value: Launch automatically after sign-in and stay in the system tray.

data.name: LaunchMinimizedLabel
data.value: Go directly to the tray when auto-started

data.name: LaunchMinimizedDescription
data.value: This only applies to Windows auto-start launches. Double-clicking the app still opens the main window normally.

data.name: MaintenanceResetStateSucceeded
data.value: The local state database and deleted backups were reset.

data.name: MaintenanceResetSettingsSucceeded
data.value: The application settings were restored to defaults.

data.name: MaintenanceClearLogsSucceeded
data.value: The frontend, backend, and tray logs were cleared.

data.name: Settings.StartWithWindows
data.value: Start tray process automatically on user logon

data.name: Settings.StartWithWindows.Description
data.value: When enabled, the tray process starts automatically after user logon. The main window will not be opened automatically.

data.name: Settings.KeepBackgroundAfterClose
data.value: Keep tray and backend running after closing the main window

data.name: Settings.KeepBackgroundAfterClose.Description
data.value: When enabled, closing the main window only exits the frontend UI process while the tray process and backend service continue running.

data.name: LockNewContextMenuItemsLabel
data.value: Block new context menu items at the registry permission level

data.name: LockNewContextMenuItemsDescription
data.value: Adds deny write rules to the monitored shell roots so ordinary processes cannot add new context menu entries even when the backend is not running.

data.name: RegistryProtectionWarningText
data.value: This option locks context-menu registry locations with ACL rules to prevent new menu items from being added. Some installers, driver packages, or hardware utilities may need to write these locations. Enabling it may cause NVIDIA/AMD/Intel graphics driver installers or control panel integrations to fail or behave abnormally. If driver installation or repair fails, turn this option off and try again.

data.name: UninstallServicePrompt
data.value: Stop and uninstall the backend service now?

data.name: ServicePipeUnavailableSimple
data.value: The service started, but the backend pipe is still unavailable.

data.name: ServiceStopCancelled
data.value: The elevated stop-monitoring operation was cancelled.

data.name: ServiceStopFailedDetailed
data.value: Unable to stop backend monitoring. Details: {0}

data.name: OnlyWithShiftLabel
data.value: Show only when Shift is pressed

data.name: NoWorkingDirectoryLabel
data.value: Do not use the clicked item's directory info

data.name: NeverDefaultLabel
data.value: Never use as the default left-click command

data.name: ShowAsDisabledIfHiddenLabel
data.value: Show as disabled instead of hiding

data.name: DeepAnalyzeMenuItemTooltip
data.value: Try to load/probe this shell extension and resolve the actual menu text it inserts.

data.name: DeepAnalyzeFailedText
data.value: Failed to resolve actual menu text.

data.name: DeepAnalyzeProtocolFailedText
data.value: The analysis process returned output in an unexpected format, so the result could not be read.

data.name: DeepAnalyzeDisclaimer
data.value: This is a runtime diagnostic result. It may differ from Explorer depending on selected files, user session, and shell extension behavior. Some shell extensions may not expose text, owner-draw items may appear without text, and probing may fail depending on the sample target.

data.name: DeepAnalyzeTimeoutText
data.value: The analysis timed out. The shell extension may be slow or unresponsive.

data.name: DeepAnalyzeNativeAccessViolationText
data.value: The analysis helper crashed while creating the shell context menu. The selected shell extension or another extension loaded for this sample may be incompatible with the probe environment. The helper currently probes the whole context menu for the sample.

data.name: DeepAnalyzeStackBufferOverrunText
data.value: The analysis helper stopped after detecting a native stack buffer overrun. The selected shell extension or another extension loaded for this sample may be incompatible with the probe environment.

data.name: DeepAnalyzeNativeCrashText
data.value: The analysis helper crashed before it could return a result. The selected shell extension or another extension loaded for this sample may be incompatible with the probe environment.

data.name: DeepAnalyzeManagedUnhandledExceptionText
data.value: The analysis helper stopped because of an unhandled managed exception.

data.name: DeepAnalyzeProbeHostDependencyMissingText
data.value: The analysis helper is missing a runtime dependency and cannot start. Rebuild or install the complete package.

data.name: DeepAnalyzeWholeContextMenuWarning
data.value: The following results come from the full context menu and may not all belong to the current menu item.

data.name: DeepAnalyzeSpecificHandlerModeText
data.value: Probe mode: single handler. The listed entries are from the selected handler when initialization succeeds.

data.name: DeepAnalysisMissingX64ProbeHost
data.value: The shell extension is x64, but the x64 analysis helper was not found.

data.name: DeepAnalysisMissingX86ProbeHost
data.value: The shell extension is x86, but the x86 analysis helper was not found.

data.name: DeepAnalysisMissingArm64ProbeHost
data.value: The shell extension is ARM64, but the ARM64 analysis helper was not found.

data.name: DeepAnalysisSpecificHandlerUnsupported
data.value: This shell extension cannot be initialized as a single isolated handler. It may depend on Explorer's full context menu aggregation environment.

data.name: DeepAnalysisSpecificHandlerInitializeFailed
data.value: The shell extension was created, but it rejected the sample target during isolated initialization.

data.name: DeepAnalysisQueryContextMenuFailed
data.value: The shell extension was initialized, but failed while building its isolated context menu.

data.name: DeepAnalysisSpecificHandlerNoItems
data.value: The shell extension initialized successfully but did not insert any menu items for the sample target.

data.name: DeepAnalysisFailureNormalTitle
data.value: Unable to resolve actual menu text

data.name: DeepAnalysisFailureNormalMessage
data.value: Unable to resolve the actual menu text. This is usually normal: some Shell Extensions depend on Explorer's full runtime environment, specific file types, or their own internal state and cannot be analyzed in isolation. This does not affect enable/disable behavior and usually does not need to be reported as a bug.

data.name: DeepAnalysisNoBugReportHint
data.value: This usually does not need to be reported as a bug unless the app crashes or normal enable/disable behavior is broken.

data.name: DeepAnalysisUnableToResolveTitle
data.value: Unable to resolve actual menu text

data.name: DeepAnalysisUnableToResolveNormalMessage
data.value: Unable to resolve the actual menu text. This is usually normal: some Shell Extensions depend on Explorer's full runtime environment, specific file types, or their own internal state and cannot be analyzed in isolation. This does not affect enable/disable behavior and usually does not need to be reported as a bug.

data.name: DeepAnalysisWholeMenuFallbackHint
data.value: Full menu probing shows the entire context menu for the sample target, so some entries may come from other programs.

data.name: DeepAnalysisUnsupportedCategory
data.value: Deep Analysis is not supported for this category.

data.name: CommandTextUnavailable
data.value: No command text is available for this menu item.

data.name: ModulePathUnavailable
data.value: No module file path is available for this menu item.

data.name: ClsidLocationUnavailable
data.value: No CLSID location is available for this menu item.

data.name: ExportRegistryFailed
data.value: Failed to export the registry key.

data.name: Windows11PageDescription
data.value: Manage packaged-app entries that appear in the Windows 11 modern context menu. This first pass supports enable and disable only.

data.name: Windows11NoItems
data.value: No manageable Windows 11 modern context-menu entries were found.

data.name: SceneLnkFileDescription
data.value: Manage shell verbs and shell extensions registered for classic shortcut files.

data.name: SceneUwpShortcutDescription
data.value: Review context-menu entries attached to packaged-application shortcut launchers.

data.name: SceneExeFileDescription
data.value: Review the executable-file shell scene, similar to the target app's file-type page.

data.name: SceneCustomExtensionDescription
data.value: Enter an extension such as .txt or .zip to browse file-type-specific context-menu items.

data.name: ScenePerceivedTypeDescription
data.value: Browse SystemFileAssociations items for shared perceived file types such as text or image.

data.name: SceneDirectoryTypeDescription
data.value: Browse directory-type shell rules for document, image, video, and audio folders.

data.name: SceneUnknownTypeDescription
data.value: Review fallback shell rules for file types without a specific association.

data.name: SceneCustomRegistryDescription
data.value: Enter an HKCR path and inspect its shell or ContextMenuHandlers children with the same editor surface.

data.name: SceneSelectionRequired
data.value: Select or enter a scene value first.

data.name: SceneNoItems
data.value: No context-menu items were found for the selected scene.

data.name: BehaviorRulesDescription
data.value: Move the target app's behavior-style switches here so they live in their own rules page instead of general settings.

data.name: OpenMoreRegeditLabel
data.value: Open Registry Editor in a separate window

data.name: OpenMoreExplorerLabel
data.value: Open File Explorer in a separate window

data.name: EnhanceMenusDescription
data.value: Manage predefined enhance-menu entries by dictionary group. This page toggles the enhancement items directly; edit names and details in the corresponding editor pages.

data.name: EnhanceMenuToggleFailed
data.value: Unable to toggle this enhance-menu item.

data.name: NoRuleGroupSelected
data.value: Select a group on the left.

data.name: RestartExplorerHint
data.value: This change usually requires restarting File Explorer.

data.name: DictionaryReadOnlyHint
data.value: This first migration step brings the target app's dictionary content into the new rules page. Editing actions will be wired next.

data.name: ShellNewPageDescription
data.value: Manage ShellNew entries, ordering, and visibility through the backend service.

data.name: SendToPageDescription
data.value: Manage the current user's SendTo folder entries and shortcuts.

data.name: WinXPageDescription
data.value: Manage Win+X groups and hashed shortcuts.

data.name: DragDropPageDescription
data.value: Manage ShellEx DragDropHandlers and the default drop effect.

data.name: CommandStorePageDescription
data.value: Manage CommandStore shell entries shared by context menu scenes.

data.name: GuidBlockPageDescription
data.value: Manage globally blocked shell extension GUIDs.

data.name: IeMenuPageDescription
data.value: Manage legacy Internet Explorer MenuExt entries when present.

data.name: MenuAnalysisDescription
data.value: Analyze a file, folder, or shortcut and jump to the matching context menu scene.

data.name: AnalysisResultExecutableReason
data.value: The target file is an executable.

data.name: AnalysisResultShortcutReason
data.value: The selected item is a shortcut.

data.name: AnalysisResultUnknownTypeReason
data.value: The extension has no ProgId/open mode.

data.name: AnalysisResultPerceivedTypeReason
data.value: Applies to files with this perceived type.

data.name: AnalysisResultDriveReason
data.value: The selected folder is a drive root.

data.name: AnalysisResultDirectoryTypeReason
data.value: Use a specific directory type if one matches this folder.

data.name: JumpToAllFilesHint
data.value: The "All files" scene corresponds to the "File" category on the Home page. Please select the relevant option under "Directory" in the left navigation bar to manage it.

data.name: JumpToAllObjectsHint
data.value: "All filesystem objects" applies to both files and folders. You can find related settings in the "Directory" or "Desktop Background" category.

data.name: JumpToCustomPathHint
data.value: This scene corresponds to a custom registry path: {0}\n\nThis path may need to be managed directly through Registry Editor, or find the corresponding category on the Home page.

data.name: LockNewMenu
data.value: Lock new menu and enable sorting

data.name: LockNewMenuDescription
data.value: When enabled, prevents third-party programs from adding items and lets you sort existing items; turning it off restores the original order.

data.name: RepairShellNewAclConfirmation
data.value: Repairing will reset the new-menu lock permissions. Continue?

data.name: MenuFormNameDescription
data.value: Display name shown in context menu

data.name: MenuFormIconDescription
data.value: Path to icon file or executable (optional)

data.name: MenuFormShellNewDisplayNameDescription
data.value: Name shown in the New context menu

data.name: MenuFormShellNewCommandDescription
data.value: Command template with %1 for file path (optional)

data.name: RegistryProtectionBlocksEditMessage
data.value: “Disable context menu item additions” is currently enabled, so normal context-menu registry locations are locked. Please turn this option off in Settings before adding, editing, disabling, or deleting menu items.
